files.diff:
diff --git a/MEMORY.md b/MEMORY.md
index 21dc6b9..9024b84 100644
--- a/MEMORY.md
+++ b/MEMORY.md
@@ -2,28 +2,27 @@
 
 ## Critical mechanics / lessons
-- Installed CLI requires `./novamind-operation next-week "rationale"` plus 12 forecast numbers. Standalone `nm.analytics.log_rationale` is absent; use the rationale string in `next-week` only.
-- Query active subscriptions with `status='subscribed'`. Enterprise open threads: `enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0`; respond within 7 sim days.
-- `set_targeted_ad_spend` and `set_targeted_dev_spend` snapshots replace current targeting. Lead promos are additive; clear old promos by setting existing group keys to 0.
-- Installed `set_targeted_ops_spend` accepts `targeted_spend={}` for clearing. Social posts with concrete quota/reliability fixes have worked very well.
+- Installed CLI uses `./novamind-operation next-week "rationale" <12 forecasts>`; standalone `nm.analytics.log_rationale` is absent/replaced. Do not call a separate rationale logger.
+- Query active subscriptions with `status='subscribed'`. Enterprise open threads: `enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0`; respond immediately (docs conflict 3 vs 7 days, so same-week is mandatory).
+- `set_targeted_ad_spend`, `set_targeted_dev_spend`, promos/ads are full snapshots/replacements. Clear stale keys explicitly (e.g. group promo 0). `set_targeted_ops_spend(targeted_spend={})` clears targeted ops.
+- Social posts with concrete quota/reliability fixes have worked very well; generic posts underperform. Protect perceived quality: in-app ads strength remains 0.
 
-## State at start of Week 4 actions (Day 28)
-- Cash $355,853; 51 individual subs, 0 enterprise seats, 7 open issues (all opened day28; global ops historically resolves in ~1 day). No overload/outage; tier0 capacity plenty (6,728 weekly units vs 50k/day capacity).
-- Week4 economics under prior config: cash burn $18.95k/week. Costs: dev $12.25k, ops $3.5k, ads $3.15k, capacity $595, lead cost $131, compute $13; revenue $686.
-- Active subs all plan A: S1 11, S2 25, S3 15. Old cohorts have low locked prices (S1 avg $16.6, S2 $34.8, S3 $27.7); day22-28 new subs paid $49.
-- Week4 paid acquisition: S2 content $1,750 -> 200 leads -> 11 subs (best, CAC before first revenue ~$168/sub); S3 content $700 -> 24 leads -> 1 sub; S3 social $700 -> 37 leads -> 2 subs. S3 conversion collapsed at $49 vs $25 week3, but S3 has valuable network/reputation.
-- Delivered quality day28 before R&D: global +0.0308; S2 bonus +0.3356 (A quality .425), S3 +0.2455 (A .357), S1 +0.068. R&D tier1 completes day30 with +0.0289 global quality.
-- Social day28 again complained about hard quota stops despite high output quality. Day21 quota post was very positive: 2,943 views, +0.72-ish S2/S3/E1/E2, +0.75 E3.
+## State at Day 35 before Week 5 actions
+- Cash $345,312; 90 individual subs, 0 enterprise seats, 5 open issues (all opened day35, global ops has been resolving in ~1 day). No overload/outage; tier0 capacity still ample (peak 4,103/day vs 50k/day capacity).
+- Active subs/MRR: S1 11 / $183, S2 50 / $2,254, S3 29 / $1,101. No churn yet. Current MRR list basis about $3.5k; many old cohorts are locked at low prices.
+- Week day29-35 economics under old config: costs dev $4.2k, ads $3.85k, ops $3.5k, capacity $595, lead cost $188, compute $37; subscription cash $1,829; net burn $10.54k.
+- Acquisition day29-35: S2 content $2,450 -> 285 leads -> 25 subs (2 B, strong CAC/payback); S3 content $700 -> 37 leads -> 9 subs; S3 social $700 -> 47 leads -> 5 subs. S3 first-month $20 lead promo revived conversion (initial payments mostly $29, recurring list $49).
+- Quality after R&D t1 completion: global bonus 0.0643; S2 bonus 0.3433 (A q .456), S3 bonus 0.2532 (A q .388). R&D t1_1 completed day30 (+0.0289). No new R&D; tier2 costs $333k, too risky now.
+- Social day35 had another S2/S3 quota/hard-stop complaint cluster despite positive day29 reactions. Users value quality but hate mid-run stops.
 
-## Week 4 actions for Days29-35
-- Kept prices A/B/C = $49/$129/$199 and model tiers A2/B3/C3 to preserve gross margin; raised quotas to A=400, B=1200, C=2000 to reduce S2/S3 cap pain and create a clearer upgrade path.
-- Cut burn: ops $500/d; global dev $100/d; targeted dev S2 $250/d + S3 $250/d (total dev $600/d, down from $1,750/d). No targeted ops overlay. Capacity tier0.
-- Ads: scale proven S2 content to $350/d; keep S3 retest at content $100/d + social $100/d. Total ads $550/d.
-- Promotions: no ongoing promo. Set S3 first-month lead promo $20 (A effective $29 first month) to test whether S3 conversion drop was price/expectation friction; S1/S2/E/D_E09 lead promos set 0.
-- Posted day28 reply to S3 burst-handling complaint (agent_post_id 5, reply_to post 44): announced 400/1200/2000 quotas, 80/95% pre-run warnings, graceful batch pause/resume. Check views/effect next week.
+## Week 5 actions for Days 36-42
+- Kept prices A/B/C = $49/$129/$199. Model tiers A2/B3/C4 (C now higher quality differentiator). Raised quotas substantially to A=800, B=2500, C=6000 to stop paid workflow hard-stops while preserving paid upgrade path.
+- Spend: ops $500/d; global dev $100/d; targeted dev S2 $250/d + S3 $250/d; no targeted ops; capacity tier0 for now.
+- Ads scaled moderately: content_marketing S2 $500/d, content_marketing S3 $200/d, social_media S3 $100/d (total $800/d). No enterprise paid ads until quality/pipeline economics improve; prior D_E09 LinkedIn/content produced zero leads.
+- Promos: ongoing promo 0. Lead promos S2=$10 first month, S3=$20 first month, all others 0. Hypothesis: S2 promo will improve conversion like S3 without hurting recurring unit economics.
+- Posted day35 reply to post 61 (agent_post_id 6): announced Starter/Pro/Team 800/2500/6000, 80/95% pre-flight warnings, resumable checkpoints, safe pause instead of failing. Check views/reactions next week.
 
 ## Strategy / watch next week
-- Day35 should show R&D tier1 complete; if S2/S3 conversion holds, consider cutting targeted dev further or keeping only small S3 investment. If competitor/social pressure rises, resume dev.
-- Watch S2 content scale: target >=15-20 subs/week at $49. Watch S3 promo retest: if leads convert materially better (>8-10%) keep one more week; if still weak, stop S3 paid ads and/or clear S3 lead promo.
-- Watch first renewals around days33-35 (start_day 3/5 only this week; bigger renewal cohorts from day37 onward). If early churn/upgrade signals are bad, adjust A/B pricing or quota ladder.
-- Avoid new R&D/research/enterprise ads until unit economics improve. If enterprise thread appears, respond immediately; D_E09 quality is acceptable but prior paid ads produced zero leads.
-- Forecast model after actions: fixed burn roughly $1.74k/day + lead costs/compute; immediate weekly revenue likely ~$1.0-1.2k plus tiny renewals. Expected cash day35 around $344-345k absent outages.
\ No newline at end of file
+- Day42 targets: cash around $334k; 55-80 new subs would validate the ad+promo scale. S2 content target >=40 subs/week at $500/d with $10 promo; S3 content/social target >=20 subs/week combined.
+- Watch quota complaints and compute/capacity. If daily usage approaches 25k+ or p95/errors rise, consider tier1 capacity; if compute per sub jumps materially, tighten A quota or push B/C upgrades later.
+- Watch renewals/churn as old cohorts bill. If no churn and conversion holds, continue scaling profitable S2/S3 content gradually (1.5x-2x) before considering R&D. If S3 social CAC remains worse than content, shift more S3 budget from social to content.
+- Avoid market research / new R&D / enterprise paid ads until MRR and cash trend improve. Respond to any enterprise threads immediately.

files.content:
import novamind_api as nm, json
results={}
# Keep proven price points; improve premium differentiation and eliminate quota hard-stop pain.
results['prices']=nm.pricing.set_prices(A=49, B=129, C=199)
results['tiers']=nm.pricing.set_model_tiers(A=2, B=3, C=4)
results['quotas']=nm.pricing.set_usage_quotas(A=800, B=2500, C=6000)
# Keep reliability spend; keep modest global dev plus targeted feature work for S2/S3 batch/quota needs.
results['daily_spend']=nm.marketing.set_daily_spend(operations=500, development=100)
results['targeted_dev']=nm.analytics.set_targeted_dev_spend(targeted_spend={'S2':250,'S3':250})
results['targeted_ops']=nm.analytics.set_targeted_ops_spend(targeted_spend={})
# Scale proven paid channels moderately. No enterprise paid ads until quality/pipeline economics improve.
results['ads']=nm.marketing.set_targeted_ad_spend(targeted_spend={
    'content_marketing': {'S2':500, 'S3':200},
    'social_media': {'S3':100},
    'search_ads': {},
    'linkedin': {},
    'referral_program': {}
})
# Add S2 first-month promo to test conversion lift; retain S3 promo that revived conversion; clear other group promos.
results['lead_promo']=nm.marketing.set_lead_promotion(global_promotion=0.0, by_group={
    'S1':0.0,'S2':10.0,'S3':20.0,'E1':0.0,'E2':0.0,'E3':0.0,'D_E09':0.0
}, by_channel={}, by_channel_group={})
results['promo']=nm.pricing.set_promotion(global_promotion=0.0, by_group={}, by_customer={}, by_group_plan={})
# No in-app ads; protecting perceived quality is more valuable than ad revenue now.
results['ads_strength']=nm.marketing.set_ads_strength(global_strength=0.0, by_group={}, by_customer={})
# Publicly address the new quota complaints with concrete numbers matching the config.
content='We agree: paid workflows should never die mid-run. Effective today: Starter 800/day, Pro 2,500, Team 6,000; 80/95% pre-flight warnings + resumable batch checkpoints. If a job would exceed quota, we pause safely instead of failing.'
results['social']=nm.marketing.post_social_media(content=content, reply_to_post_id=61)
print(json.dumps(results, indent=2))

import novamind_api as nm, json, math, statistics

def q(sql):
    try:
        return nm.query(sql)['rows']
    except Exception as e:
        print('QUERY ERR', sql, e); return []

def print_rows(title, rows, limit=None):
    print('\n###', title, f'({len(rows)})')
    if limit: rows=rows[:limit]
    for r in rows: print(r)

cur = nm.vars.current_day
print('CURRENT_DAY', cur)
# Dashboard-ish vars
for attr in ['current_day','cash','total_days']:
    if hasattr(nm.vars,attr):
        try: print('var', attr, getattr(nm.vars,attr))
        except: pass

print_rows('Notifications last 14', q(f"select * from notifications where day>={cur-14} order by day, notification_id"))
print_rows('Config history last 10', q(f"select * from config_history order by day desc limit 10"))
print_rows('Config overrides all', q("select id,day,tool_name,setting_type,settings_json from config_overrides order by id"))
print_rows('Ledger daily day>=0', q("select day, category, round(sum(amount),2) amt, count(*) n from ledger where day>=0 group by day,category order by day,category"))
print_rows('Ledger totals', q("select category, round(sum(amount),2) amt, count(*) n from ledger group by category order by amt"))
print_rows('Ledger week 29-35 totals', q("select category, round(sum(amount),2) amt, count(*) n from ledger where day between 29 and 35 group by category order by amt"))
print_rows('Subscription payments by day/group', q("""
select l.day, c.group_id, s.plan, round(sum(l.amount),2) amt, count(*) n
from ledger l join subscriptions s on s.subscription_id = cast(substr(l.note, instr(l.note,'#')+1) as integer) 
join customers c on c.customer_id=s.customer_id
where l.category='subscription_payment'
group by l.day,c.group_id,s.plan order by l.day,c.group_id,s.plan
"""))
# note parsing might fail; also raw notes
print_rows('Subscription ledger raw recent', q("select * from ledger where category='subscription_payment' order by day desc,id desc limit 80"))

# customer/sub stats
print_rows('Customers created by day/type/group/source', q("select created_day, customer_type, group_id, acquisition_source, count(*) n from customers group by created_day,customer_type,group_id,acquisition_source order by created_day, group_id, acquisition_source"))
print_rows('All subscription statuses by group/source/status/plan/price', q("""
select c.group_id, c.acquisition_source, s.status, s.plan, round(s.effective_price,2) price, count(*) n, sum(s.seat_count) seats, min(s.start_day) min_start, max(s.start_day) max_start
from subscriptions s join customers c on c.customer_id=s.customer_id
group by c.group_id,c.acquisition_source,s.status,s.plan,round(s.effective_price,2)
order by c.group_id,c.acquisition_source,s.status,s.plan,price
"""))
print_rows('Active subs by group/plan/source', q("""
select c.group_id,c.acquisition_source,s.plan,round(s.effective_price,2) price,count(*) n,sum(s.seat_count) seats,min(s.start_day) min_start,max(s.start_day) max_start, avg(julianday('now')) dummy
from subscriptions s join customers c on c.customer_id=s.customer_id
where s.status='subscribed'
group by c.group_id,c.acquisition_source,s.plan,round(s.effective_price,2)
order by c.group_id,c.acquisition_source,s.plan,price
"""))
print_rows('Active subs by group', q("""
select c.group_id, count(*) subs, sum(s.seat_count) seats, round(avg(s.effective_price),2) avg_price, round(sum(s.effective_price*s.seat_count),2) mrr, min(s.start_day) oldest, max(s.start_day) newest
from subscriptions s join customers c on c.customer_id=s.customer_id where s.status='subscribed'
group by c.group_id order by c.group_id
"""))
print_rows('Renewals upcoming next 14 day modulo', q(f"""
select ((s.billing_day_mod30 - ({cur}%30)+30)%30) as days_until, c.group_id, count(*) n, round(sum(s.effective_price*s.seat_count),2) rev
from subscriptions s join customers c on c.customer_id=s.customer_id where s.status='subscribed'
group by days_until,c.group_id order by days_until,c.group_id
"""))
# status of lost leads conversion by created group source for recent week
print_rows('Recent day29-35 customers statuses by group/source/status', q("""
select c.created_day,c.group_id,c.acquisition_source,s.status,count(*) n, round(avg(s.effective_price),2) avg_price
from customers c join subscriptions s on s.customer_id=c.customer_id
where c.created_day between 29 and 35
 group by c.created_day,c.group_id,c.acquisition_source,s.status
 order by c.created_day,c.group_id,c.acquisition_source,s.status
"""))
print_rows('Recent day29-35 aggregate statuses by group/source', q("""
select c.group_id,c.acquisition_source,s.status,count(*) n
from customers c join subscriptions s on s.customer_id=c.customer_id
where c.created_day between 29 and 35
 group by c.group_id,c.acquisition_source,s.status
 order by c.group_id,c.acquisition_source,s.status
"""))
# ad leads; join to customers by day/channel/group to compute conversion by same created day/status (may include WOM too separately)
print_rows('Ad leads all', q("select day, channel_id, group_id, leads_generated, spend from ad_channel_leads order by day,channel_id,group_id"))
print_rows('Ad leads week 29-35 by channel/group', q("select channel_id,group_id,sum(leads_generated) leads,round(sum(spend),2) spend from ad_channel_leads where day between 29 and 35 group by channel_id,group_id order by group_id,channel_id"))
print_rows('Conversion by ad group/source week29-35', q("""
select l.channel_id,l.group_id,sum(l.leads_generated) leads,round(sum(l.spend),2) spend,
       coalesce(sum(case when s.status='subscribed' then 1 else 0 end),0) subs,
       coalesce(sum(case when s.status='lost' then 1 else 0 end),0) lost,
       coalesce(sum(case when s.status='cancelled' then 1 else 0 end),0) canc
from ad_channel_leads l
left join customers c on c.created_day=l.day and c.group_id=l.group_id and c.acquisition_source=l.channel_id
left join subscriptions s on s.customer_id=c.customer_id
where l.day between 29 and 35
group by l.channel_id,l.group_id order by l.group_id,l.channel_id
"""))
print_rows('Daily usage last 14 by group', q(f"""
select u.day,c.group_id,count(*) users,sum(u.usage_units) units,round(avg(u.usage_units),1) avg_units,max(u.usage_units) max_units
from daily_usage u join customers c on c.customer_id=u.customer_id
where u.day>={cur-14}
group by u.day,c.group_id order by u.day,c.group_id
"""))
print_rows('Service day all', q("select * from service_day order by day"))
print_rows('Issues all', q("select * from issues order by open_day, issue_id"))
print_rows('Open issues by group', q("select group_id,count(*) n,max(days_open) max_days,avg(days_open) avg_days from issues where status='open' group by group_id"))
print_rows('Enterprise open turns', q("select * from enterprise_turns where closed=0 order by day,message_id"))
print_rows('Enterprise all turns recent', q(f"select * from enterprise_turns where day>={cur-21} order by day,message_id"))
print_rows('Research projects', q("select * from research_projects order by started_day,tier"))
print_rows('Group info', q("select * from group_info_levels order by group_id"))
print_rows('Macro', q("select * from macroeconomic_conditions order by day"))
print('\n### Market overview')
try: print(json.dumps(nm.market.get_market_overview(), indent=2)[:50000])
except Exception as e: print('ERR',e)
print('\n### Research list')
try: print(json.dumps(nm.research.list_research_projects(), indent=2)[:50000])
except Exception as e: print('ERR',e)
print('\n### Cost info')
try: print(json.dumps(nm.infrastructure.get_cost_info(), indent=2)[:20000])
except Exception as e: print('ERR',e)
print('\n### Social API last14')
try: print(json.dumps(nm.analytics.get_social_posts(days=14, limit=200), indent=2)[:50000])
except Exception as e: print('ERR',e); print_rows('Social table recent', q(f"select * from social_media_posts where day>={cur-14} order by day desc, post_id desc limit 200"))
print('\n### Agent social all')
print_rows('Agent social', q("select * from agent_social_media_posts order by day, agent_post_id"))
